title: stachys alopecuros subsp. divulsa
intro: Stachys alopecuros subsp. divulsa, also known as Spanish hyssop or tall betony, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Stachys alopecuros subsp. divulsa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe sore throats, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are prepared by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
